response.justification: While James is an accomplished software engineer with broad experience, he is fundamentally misaligned with this embedded networking security role. Most critically, he has no native Japanese language proficiency, which is an explicit hard requirement. His career focuses on web and modern full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than embedded C/UNIX development. He lacks demonstrated expertise in the security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, encryption), networking protocols (SNMP, NETCONF, switch configuration), and embedded firmware domains that are central to this position.
strengths: Proven ability to master multiple programming languages and rapidly learn new technical domains across diverse projects
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and architectural thinking demonstrated across all roles
Some embedded systems experience with hardware interaction including robotic arms and mobile device orchestration tools
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency (explicitly required) and no indication of any Japanese ability at any level
Limited recent C/UNIX experience—only 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015)—with primary focus on modern languages like TypeScript and DLang
Zero experience in required security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X), networking protocols (SNMP, NETCONF), or embedded firmware development
